title: 13.12 — Member selection with pointers and references

content.text: Best practiceWhen using a pointer to access a member, use the member selection from pointer operator (->) instead of the member selection operator (.).
When using a pointer to access a member, use the member selection from pointer operator (->) instead of the member selection operator (.).

Note that in the case of(ptr->paw).claws, parentheses aren’t necessary since bothoperator->andoperator.evaluate in left to right order, but it does help readability slightly.Next lesson13.13Class templatesBack to table of contentsPrevious lesson13.11Struct miscellany
